upload.descriptionMiddle: and select "Download larger data archive" (the first option on that page). Upload the ZIP file you receive (batch 1 arrives in your email inbox in ~10 minutes).

upload.privacyNote: Your LinkedIn ZIP is extracted in your browser and never leaves your device. The structured profile data is then written directly to your Personal Data Server through our API.
upload.publicDataNotice: Everything you import will be publicly visible to anyone, including people without a Sifa account. Unlike LinkedIn (where some data is hidden from logged-out visitors), Sifa profiles are fully public. Review your data before importing.
upload.reimportBody: Importing again will fully replace your existing positions, education, skills, and other LinkedIn-imported data with the contents of this new export. Items you removed from LinkedIn will also be removed from Sifa. Items you added or edited directly on Sifa will be overwritten. You can review and remove individual items before confirming in the next step.

preview.existingBody: Importing will replace all existing profile data with the data below. Your profile headline and summary will also be updated.
preview.publicDataNotice: Everything you import will be publicly visible to anyone, including people without a Sifa account. Unlike LinkedIn (where some data is hidden from logged-out visitors), Sifa profiles are fully public. Review your data before importing.

confirm.writingRecords: Writing {count} records to your Personal Data Server...
confirm.formattingHint: LinkedIn exports strip text formatting. We did a first pass to restore paragraph breaks and bullet points, but some descriptions may still need manual cleanup. Review your profile and edit any sections that look off.

sections.verifyHintGithub: Add your Sifa profile URL in the "URL" field or under "Social accounts" in your GitHub profile settings. We'll verify the link automatically when you save.
sections.verifyHintWebsite: Add the following tag to your site's head section. We'll verify the link automatically when you save.
sections.verifyHintFediverse: Add your Sifa profile URL to one of your Fediverse profile metadata fields. We'll verify the link automatically when you save.
sections.verifyHintRss: Add a link with rel="me" pointing to your Sifa profile URL on your feed's HTML page. We'll verify the link automatically when you save.
sections.verifyHintOrcid: Your ORCID publications will automatically appear in your Publications section. To verify ownership, add your Sifa profile URL as a website in your ORCID researcher-urls section, then use the "Check now" button.

login.title: Get started on Sifa
login.description: Your professional profile, owned by you.
login.newUserExplanation: Sifa stores your career data in your own account, not on our servers. Pick a provider below to create your free account. It takes two minutes and you'll land right back here.
login.welcomeBackDescription: Enter the handle you just created to sign in.
login.welcomeBackNotReady: Still setting up? Go back to the providers above.
login.providerRecommendation: Not sure which to pick? Choose Bluesky, it's the most popular. You can always switch later.
login.whyExternalHeading: Why do I need to create an account somewhere else?
login.whyExternalExplanation: Sifa is built on the AT Protocol, an open standard that keeps your identity separate from any single app. Your account works across Sifa, Bluesky, and dozens of other apps. You own your data, and no platform can lock you out of it.
login.handleLabel: Your Bluesky or AT Protocol handle

about.title: About Sifa ID
about.intro: Sifa ID is a professional identity network built on the AT Protocol, the same open network behind Bluesky. Your career history, skills, and endorsements live in your own data server, not in ours. Switch providers, leave Sifa, or build your own client. Your data stays yours.
about.why: LinkedIn controls the professional identity of close to a billion people. If they lock your account, your career history disappears. If they change their API, every tool that depends on it breaks. Professional identity is too important to live inside one company's database.
about.how: Sifa reads and writes professional profile data to your AT Protocol Personal Data Server (PDS). We provide the interface: the import tools, the profile pages, the endorsement system. The data is always yours, stored where you choose, portable to any compatible app.
about.name: Sifa is Swahili for "praise, reputation, and qualification." It's part of Singi Labs (from msingi, "foundation"), alongside Barazo (from baraza, "community council"). The three names match what we're building: community-owned professional infrastructure, from the ground up.
about.founderBio: , a community and conversion specialist based in the Netherlands. Guido has spent two decades connecting technology and community, from the Joomla community in 2005 to the Magento ecosystem and developer relations at companies like Euroflorist and Spryker. Sifa grew out of a simple frustration: why does a platform own your professional reputation when the technology exists to own it yourself?
about.openSource: Sifa's professional profile lexicons and import tools are open source under the MIT license. The application code is source-available on GitHub. Professional identity standards should be open to everyone, even our competitors.
about.cta: If you have a Bluesky or AT Protocol account, you already have a Sifa profile waiting. Sign in, import your LinkedIn data, and own your professional identity.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.introSuffix: and based in the Netherlands. We built Sifa on the AT Protocol because professional identity data shouldn't be controlled by a single platform. This policy explains what data we handle and how.
privacy.dataStorageBody: Your professional profile data is stored in your AT Protocol Personal Data Server (PDS), not on Sifa's servers. You control your data and can move it to any compatible provider at any time. Sifa reads from and writes to your PDS on your behalf. We don't maintain a separate copy.
privacy.importBody: When you import data from LinkedIn, the ZIP file is extracted entirely in your browser. It never leaves your device. The structured profile data is then written directly to your Personal Data Server through our API. We don't store, log, or inspect the contents of your LinkedIn export.
privacy.hostingBody: All Sifa infrastructure runs in the EU. Our servers are hosted by Hetzner in Germany. Your PDS may be hosted anywhere you choose. Because profile data lives in your PDS, Sifa doesn't determine where your data is stored. You do.
privacy.euCommitmentBody: We pick EU-based providers or self-host open source tools on our own EU infrastructure rather than route your traffic through US companies like Google or Cloudflare. Our analytics run on Umami, self-hosted in the Netherlands. Our website production servers are hosted by Hetzner in Germany.
privacy.analyticsBody: We use Umami for website analytics. Umami is open source, cookieless, and fully GDPR-compliant. We self-host it on our own infrastructure in the Netherlands — no data is sent to third parties. Umami collects only aggregate, anonymous usage data such as page views, referrer URLs, browser type, and country. It does not track individual users, use cookies, or collect any personal information. You can verify this yourself: Umami's source code is public at github.com/umami-software/umami.
privacy.cookiesBody: Sifa uses a session cookie for authentication and a preference cookie for dark/light mode. That's the complete list. No tracking cookies, no analytics cookies, no third-party advertising cookies. Our analytics tool (Umami) is fully cookieless.
privacy.activityBody: If you claim your Sifa profile, we scan your AT Protocol Personal Data Server to detect which AT Protocol apps you use (such as Bluesky, Tangled, or Whitewind). We store lightweight usage statistics — which apps you're active on and approximate record counts — in our database. We do not store your posts, code, photos, or other content. Activity content is read live from your PDS when someone views your profile and cached temporarily (up to 15 minutes).
privacy.activityLegalBasis: Legal basis: legitimate interest (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)). You actively claimed your profile and were informed about this processing. You can hide any app from your profile in your activity settings, or request full removal at any time.
privacy.activityUnclaimedBody: If you have not claimed your Sifa profile, we do not collect or display any cross-app activity data. Unclaimed profile pages show only basic identity information from the AT Protocol (handle, display name, avatar).
privacy.activityVisibilityBody: You can hide specific apps from your Sifa profile in your activity settings. This controls what Sifa displays — it does not delete or hide data from the AT Protocol itself. To remove data from the protocol, delete it in the app where you originally posted it.
privacy.removalBody: Any AT Protocol user can request removal of their profile and activity data from Sifa, even without a Sifa account. Visit sifa.id/privacy/removal to submit a request. We delete all stored statistics, purge cached data, and permanently prevent your profile from being re-created on Sifa. Your data in your PDS is not affected.
privacy.rightsBody: Under the GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, and object to the processing of your personal data. Your profile data lives in your PDS — you can inspect, edit, and delete it directly. For data Sifa stores (usage statistics, visibility preferences), contact us or use the removal request form. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens).
privacy.loggingBody: We log server errors and API request metadata (timestamps, response codes) for debugging. Logs don't contain profile data and are rotated automatically.
privacy.contactBody: For privacy questions, reach us at privacy@sifa.id.

dataTransparency.title: Your data on the AT Protocol
dataTransparency.body: Your professional profile is stored in your Personal Data Server (PDS). This data is public and portable. You can inspect exactly what is stored and take it to any compatible provider.

embedBuilder.pageTitle: Embed a Sifa ID Profile Card
embedBuilder.pageDescription: Embed a live Sifa ID professional profile card on any website. Auto-updates when the profile changes. Free, no account required.
embedBuilder.pageSubtitle: Add a live professional profile card to any website with a single line of code.
embedBuilder.themeNote: The embed automatically adjusts between light and dark mode based on the visitor's browser setting.
embedBuilder.enterHandle: Enter a handle or DID to see a preview
embedBuilder.alwaysUpToDateDescription: The embed pulls live data from your Sifa profile and AT Protocol activity. When you update your profile, the embed updates too. No manual changes needed.
embedBuilder.anyProfileDescription: Embed any Sifa profile, not just your own. Build a team page, speaker directory, or community member list. See the <example>ATmosphere Conf 2026 speaker directory</example> for an example.
embedBuilder.comingSoonDescription: This is a first version of the embed. In a future update, you'll be able to choose which elements appear on the card, like toggling skills, location, or activity badges on and off.

notFound.easterEggMessage: You found the secret! Sifa means "praise" in Swahili. Consider yourself praised for your curiosity.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.introSuffix: and based in the Netherlands. By using Sifa, you agree to the following terms.
terms.serviceBody: Sifa provides a web interface for managing professional profiles on the AT Protocol. Your profile data is stored in your Personal Data Server (PDS), not on Sifa's servers. We provide the tools to create, edit, and display that data. We don't control it.
terms.accountsBody: You sign in with your existing AT Protocol account. Sifa doesn't create or manage accounts. Your identity is managed by your PDS provider (such as Bluesky). If you delete your AT Protocol account, your Sifa profile goes with it.
terms.dataBody: All professional profile data you create through Sifa is written to your PDS. You can export, move, or delete it at any time through your PDS provider. If Sifa shuts down, your data stays in your PDS, accessible through any compatible client.
terms.contentBody: You're responsible for the accuracy and legality of the profile information you publish. Sifa may remove content from its interface that violates applicable law or community guidelines. Removal from Sifa doesn't delete data from your PDS.
terms.disclaimerBody: Sifa is provided as-is during the alpha period. We don't guarantee uptime or feature stability. Your data in your PDS stays under your control regardless of Sifa's availability. If something breaks, your professional identity doesn't break with it.

endorsement.endorsementContext: How do you know this person's expertise?

roadmap.disclaimer: This roadmap reflects our current thinking and priorities. Items may shift in order, change scope, or be replaced as we learn more. No dates are promised.
roadmap.contributeBody: Sifa is built in the open. If you have an idea, ran into a bug, or want to contribute code, we'd love to hear from you.

dangerZone.title: Danger Zone
dangerZone.resetDescription: Deletes all Sifa profile data (positions, education, skills, and all other sections) from Sifa and your Personal Data Server, then takes you to the import page to start over. Your Sifa settings, AT Protocol account, Bluesky profile, posts, follows, and data from other apps are not affected.
dangerZone.deleteDescription: Removes the same Sifa profile data as Reset Profile, plus your Sifa settings (notifications, content filters, and similar preferences), then signs you out. Your AT Protocol account, Bluesky profile, posts, follows, and data from other apps are not affected. To delete your AT Protocol account entirely, visit your PDS provider's settings.
dangerZone.deleteModalTitle: Your Sifa account has been deleted.
dangerZone.deleteModalBody: Your AT Protocol account and Personal Data Server still exist. Sifa only removed its own data. If you want to delete your AT Protocol account entirely, visit your PDS provider's settings.
dangerZone.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

privacyRemoval.title: Request Profile Removal
privacyRemoval.description: You can request the removal of your profile and activity data from Sifa. This removes your data from our database and prevents Sifa from displaying or scanning your profile in the future. The data itself remains in your AT Protocol account. Sifa cannot modify or delete your PDS records.
privacyRemoval.inputLabel: Your AT Protocol handle or DID
privacyRemoval.successMessage: Your removal request has been received. Your profile will be removed within 72 hours.
privacyRemoval.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again or contact privacy@sifa.id.
privacyRemoval.whatGetsRemoved: Your profile page, activity statistics, and cached data on Sifa.
privacyRemoval.whatStays: Your data in your AT Protocol Personal Data Server (PDS). To delete data from the protocol, use the app where you originally posted it.

activitySettings.notOwnProfile: You can only manage activity visibility for your own profile.

compareLinkedin.kicker: Your professional data belongs to you
compareLinkedin.title: The LinkedIn alternative that gives you your data back
compareLinkedin.description: LinkedIn alternative with data ownership, Sifa ID
compareLinkedin.metaDescription: LinkedIn owns your career data. Sifa ID stores it in your own account, portable and yours to keep. No ads, no lock-in. See the full comparison.
compareLinkedin.subhead: Your career history, skills, and endorsements live in your own data account, not on someone else's servers. Portable, verifiable, yours to keep. Built in the Netherlands, hosted in Germany, funded by no one but ourselves.
compareLinkedin.heroCta: Import your LinkedIn profile in 2 minutes

data.label: Your data
data.linkedin: Stored on LinkedIn's servers. You can export a copy, but the live profile and its network context stay behind.
data.sifa: Stored in your own data account. Move it, delete it, or take it to another app anytime.

portability.label: Profile portability
portability.linkedin: Leave LinkedIn, lose your live profile. The export is a static archive.
portability.sifa: Take your profile to any compatible app. If Sifa disappears, your data doesn't.

signIn.label: Sign-in
signIn.linkedin: One LinkedIn account, one platform. LinkedIn decides if you keep access.
signIn.sifa: Sign in with your Bluesky account (or any AT Protocol account). No Sifa-specific signup needed.

algorithm.label: Algorithm
algorithm.linkedin: LinkedIn's algorithm controls your feed and who sees your profile in search. It's optimized for engagement, not for you.
algorithm.sifa: No algorithmic feed. Your profile is publicly searchable by default.

privacy.label: Privacy
privacy.linkedin: Activity tracked for Microsoft's advertising network. US-headquartered, subject to US data laws.
privacy.sifa: No tracking, no ads. EU-hosted (Hetzner, Germany) with cookieless analytics (Umami, self-hosted in the Netherlands).

jurisdiction.label: Jurisdiction
jurisdiction.linkedin: Owned by Microsoft (US). Your data falls under US jurisdiction, including the CLOUD Act. EU data residency is available, but a US parent company still governs processing.
jurisdiction.sifa: Built and operated from the Netherlands, all infrastructure in the EU (Germany). No US parent company. GDPR is the only law that applies.

endorsements.label: Endorsements
endorsements.linkedin: Endorsements from people who may never have seen your work, with no verification of the relationship.
endorsements.sifa: Endorsements require mutual confirmation and include context about how the endorser knows your work.

profileViews.label: Profile views
profileViews.sifa: Free, with a mutual privacy model: you see views only if you share yours too.

cost.label: Cost
cost.linkedin: Free tier with persistent upselling. Premium starts at ~$30/month.
cost.sifa: Free. No premium tiers, no upselling.

sourceCode.label: Source code
sourceCode.sifa: Source-available on GitHub. Professional profile data standards are MIT-licensed.

crossApp.label: Cross-app activity
crossApp.sifa: Shows your activity across the open social web: Bluesky posts, code contributions, blog posts, event attendance.

networkSize.label: Network size
networkSize.linkedin: ~1 billion members. The largest professional network by far.
networkSize.sifa: Early stage, growing within the AT Protocol community. Your Bluesky connections carry over.

jobBoard.label: Job board
jobBoard.linkedin: Massive job marketplace with recruiter tools, InMail, and applicant tracking.
jobBoard.sifa: Not yet. Job board integration is on the roadmap.

companyPages.label: Company pages
companyPages.linkedin: Full company profiles with employee listings, company updates, and hiring pages.
companyPages.sifa: Not yet. Company pages are planned.

compareLinkedin.acknowledgment: LinkedIn has a billion members, two decades of recruiter tooling, and a job marketplace we haven't built yet. We're not trying to replace all of that. Sifa exists for a different reason: to give you a professional identity that you actually control. The job board and company pages are on <roadmapLink>the roadmap</roadmapLink>.
compareLinkedin.whenLinkedinTitle: When LinkedIn is the better choice

whenLinkedin.recruiting: You're actively job hunting and need recruiter inbox access
whenLinkedin.industry: Your industry's hiring happens primarily on LinkedIn
whenLinkedin.companyPages: You need company pages or LinkedIn Learning
whenLinkedin.network: Your entire professional network is on LinkedIn with no interest in moving

compareLinkedin.diffProfile: Your Sifa profile is a public, SEO-indexed page that works on its own, even if nobody else is on Sifa yet. Embed it on your personal website or conference speaker page with a single line of code. Already on LinkedIn? Import your career history in 2 minutes and own it from here on.
compareLinkedin.diffEndorsements: Each Sifa endorsement records how the endorser knows your work: "worked together at [company]", "collaborated in [community]", "co-authored [project]". Both sides confirm before it shows on your profile. No more meaningless +1s from strangers.
compareLinkedin.diffEuropeTitle: Built in Europe, not bolted on
compareLinkedin.diffEurope: LinkedIn is owned by Microsoft, headquartered in the US. Even with EU data centers, your professional data is governed by a US parent company subject to the CLOUD Act. Sifa is built in the Netherlands, hosted in Germany, with no US parent entity. We use EU-based or self-hosted services for everything: analytics, error tracking, email. We build around GDPR from day one.
compareLinkedin.diffPaywalls: Who viewed your profile? Free. Full search? Free. No "premium" tier, no InMail credits, no connection limits. Your profile is publicly findable and no algorithm decides who sees it.

faq.importQ: Can I move my existing LinkedIn profile to Sifa?
faq.importA: Yes. Export your LinkedIn data, upload the ZIP to Sifa, and your positions, education, and skills are imported in about 2 minutes. The ZIP is processed in your browser and never leaves your device.
faq.bothQ: Does Sifa work if I still want to use LinkedIn?
faq.bothA: Yes. Sifa gives you a portable version of your professional identity that you control, regardless of what happens on any platform.
faq.freeA: Yes. Profiles, search, endorsements, and profile analytics are all free. There are no premium tiers.
faq.pdsQ: What is a Personal Data Server?
faq.pdsA: A Personal Data Server (PDS) is where your data lives on the AT Protocol, the open standard behind Bluesky. Think of it as your own account that you control. You can move it between providers or even host it yourself.
faq.storedA: Your profile data is stored in your Personal Data Server, which you choose. Sifa's application servers are hosted by Hetzner in Germany, under EU data protection law.
faq.usLawQ: Is Sifa subject to US data laws?
faq.usLawA: No. Sifa is operated from the Netherlands with servers in Germany. There's no US parent company. Your data is governed exclusively by EU law (GDPR). We use EU-based or self-hosted tools for all infrastructure, so no data flows to US services like Google Analytics, Cloudflare, or AWS.
faq.jobBoardQ: Does Sifa have a job board?
faq.jobBoardA: Not yet. A job board integration is on the roadmap. Right now Sifa focuses on professional identity: your profile, endorsements, and cross-app activity.

compareLinkedin.ctaTitle: Ready to own your professional identity?

connect.pageDescription: Scan the QR code to connect with {name} on Sifa ID.

handshake.subtitle: Have them scan this with Sifa to confirm you met.
handshake.profileQrLink: Need a QR for slides? Get a static profile link
handshake.privacyNotice: Stored in both AT Protocol accounts as a verified IRL connection.
handshake.privacyNoticeDetail: Each handshake is unique per person and stored as public data in both users' AT Protocol accounts. Confirmed handshakes appear as verified IRL connections in your My Network tab.
handshake.confirmedSubtitle: You and {name} now have a verified meeting on record.
handshake.alreadyMetSubtitle: You and {name} confirmed a meeting on {date}.
handshake.signInSubtitle: Sign in to confirm you met {name}. This creates a verified IRL connection that appears in both your networks.
handshake.justFollow: Just want to follow? View their profile instead.
handshake.selfScan: That's your own code! Show it to someone you're meeting.
handshake.expired: This code has expired. Ask them to refresh their screen.
handshake.usedToken: This code has already been used. Ask them to tap New code.
handshake.rateLimited: Too many handshakes (limit: 30/hour). Try again later.
handshake.addNotePrompt: Want to add a note about meeting {name}?
handshake.eventContext: You both had an RSVP for {event} that day
handshake.eventContextSelf: You had an RSVP for {event} that day
handshake.explainerStep1: Tap Meet on your profile, or go to sifa.id/meet
handshake.explainerStep2: Show your QR code to someone you're talking to
handshake.explainerStep3: They scan it with their phone camera
handshake.explainerStep4: Both of you confirm and you're connected as a verified IRL meeting
handshake.scopeInsufficientSubtitle: Handshake needs extra permissions to write verified in-person meeting records to your AT Protocol account. You'll be redirected to your identity provider to approve.

peopleNotes.notePlaceholder: e.g. Talked about AT Protocol trust systems at the Drizzle booth
peopleNotes.notePrivacy: This data will be stored securely on Sifa servers and NOT in your public PDS. But still, don't write things in here that would be awkward if they came out.

stats.title: ATProto stats for nerds
stats.subtitle: Live statistics from the AT Protocol's PLC directory, the public ledger where every did:plc identity is registered.
stats.totalDidsTooltip: Total did:plc entries registered in the PLC directory since November 2022. Includes all accounts from all PDS providers, whether active or not.
stats.reachableDidsTooltip: Identities whose handle resolves back to their DID. This means the account's PDS is online and the handle is properly configured. Estimated from samples per PDS provider.
stats.pdsHostsTooltip: Unique Personal Data Server endpoints that have registered at least one did:plc identity. A PDS stores user data and can be self-hosted by anyone.
stats.lastUpdatedTooltip: When this data was last refreshed from the PLC directory.
stats.methodologyPlc: The Public Ledger of Credentials is the registry where AT Protocol identities are created. Every did:plc identity has a signed genesis operation recorded here. The directory is currently operated by Bluesky PBC, with plans to transfer to an independent organization.
stats.methodologyPds: A Personal Data Server stores your AT Protocol data: posts, profile, follows, and more. Bluesky runs the largest PDS cluster (the mushroom-named servers like morel, cortinarius, etc.), but anyone can run their own.
stats.methodologyReachable: We sample handles from each PDS and check if they resolve back to their DID via the AT Protocol's identity system. If the handle resolves, the account is functional. Most unreachable identities come from spam PDS hosts that mass-register DIDs without serving real content (e.g. pds.trump.com registered over 17 million DIDs with 0% reachability). Other reasons include deleted or suspended accounts, PDS hosts that went offline, or DIDs that were never properly set up.
stats.methodologyCollection: We crawl the PLC directory's public export API daily, counting new identity registrations per PDS provider. The crawl uses the plc.wtf community mirror to avoid impacting the primary directory. Reachability is checked by sampling handles and resolving them via the public API.
stats.methodologyComparisonTitle: Why do these numbers differ from other trackers?
stats.methodologyComparisonBefore: You may notice different numbers on other AT Protocol trackers like
stats.methodologyComparisonAfter: . The difference comes down to what gets counted. We count all identities registered in the PLC directory, including those on spam PDS hosts and inactive accounts. Other trackers typically query each PDS directly and only count accounts marked as active, which excludes deleted, suspended, and deactivated accounts. Neither approach is wrong; they measure different things. We show total registrations and reachability to give a complete picture of the network, including its spam problem.
